title: Seven Gifts from the Volcano's Edge
synopsis: When a young chameleon's rainbow maker breaks on the volcanic rim where he lives, seven mysterious gifts arrive over the course of a week, each one revealing new secrets about light, color, and the magic of making things work again.

pages.text: Steam puffs and hisses from the black sand where the rainbow maker lies broken. CRACK! CLATTER! The crystal pieces sparkle-scatter across the volcanic rim like fallen stars. Research Subject: Mango, a chameleon with jewel-bright green-and-orange scales, independently swiveling eyes, a coiled tail, and sticky toes. Observation: The little chameleon sits very still, both eyes swiveling to examine each broken shard. His scales shimmer from bright orange to deep blue—the color of sadness, I note. 'Pop-pop-POP!' go the steam vents nearby, as if the sleeping volcano is trying to cheer him up. But rainbows need light and crystal working together, and this crystal is split-split-SPLIT into a hundred pieces.

illustration.description: A chameleon with jewel-bright green-and-orange scales, independently swiveling eyes, a coiled tail, and sticky toes examines broken crystal pieces scattered on black volcanic sand. Steam rises from volcanic vents in the background. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Mango looking sadly at his broken rainbow maker on the volcanic rim

pages.text: Then—THUD! A small red box tumbles down the volcanic slope, bounce-bounce-bouncing until it lands near Mango's sticky toes. Field Note: Most unusual! No wind today, yet this object appeared from nowhere. The little chameleon's left eye examines the box while his right eye still watches the broken crystal. His scales flicker curious-yellow. Inside the red box: one perfect ruby lens, round and smooth. When Mango holds it up to the morning sun, red light dances across the black sand like fire-sprites. 'Ooooh!' he whispers, both eyes now focused forward in wonder. Research Question: Where did this gift come from? The volcano rim stretches empty in all directions, just black sand and puffing steam vents.

illustration.description: A chameleon with jewel-bright green-and-orange scales, independently swiveling eyes, a coiled tail, and sticky toes holds up a ruby lens to the sun, creating red light patterns on black volcanic sand. A small red box sits nearby. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Mango discovering the first gift and the magic of the ruby lens

chapters.title: Days Two Through Four: Orange, Yellow, Green

pages.text: Day Two Field Notes: BONK! Another box—orange this time—rolls down the slope at sunrise. Inside: an amber lens that makes golden light dance-prance across the sand. Mango's scales turn happy-green as he holds both lenses together. Orange light plus red light equals... 'Whoa-whoa-WHOA!' Day Three: A yellow box arrives with a THUMP-THUMP-THUMP! The citrine lens inside makes sunshine-bright beams that make the chameleon giggle-wiggle. His coiled tail uncurls with excitement. Day Four brings a jade-green box that tumble-rumbles down like the others. The emerald lens creates forest-green light that reminds Mango of the leaves he used to hide in, far from this black-sand home.

illustration.description: A chameleon with jewel-bright green-and-orange scales, independently swiveling eyes, a coiled tail, and sticky toes sits surrounded by four colorful boxes (red, orange, yellow, green) with various colored lenses scattered around. Each lens creates different colored light beams on the black volcanic sand. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Mango surrounded by the first four gift boxes and their magical lenses

pages.text: Research Observation: The little chameleon has begun to experiment! He holds two lenses together—red and yellow make orange light splash across the sand. Yellow and green create a lime-bright glow that makes his eyes widen in surprise. 'Mix-mix-MIX!' he chatters happily, scales shifting through every color like a living rainbow. But still, something is missing-missing-MISSING. The broken crystal pieces still lie scattered, and these beautiful lights can't make rainbows without the right way to bend them. Pop-pop-POP go the steam vents, keeping steady rhythm like a patient heartbeat from deep inside the sleeping volcano. Three more gifts to come, if the pattern holds true.

illustration.description: A chameleon with jewel-bright green-and-orange scales, independently swiveling eyes, a coiled tail, and sticky toes experiments with overlapping colored lenses, creating mixed light patterns. His scales are changing colors rapidly. Steam vents puff in the background on black sand. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Mango experimenting with mixing colored lights from the lenses

chapters.title: Days Five Through Seven: The Rainbow Returns

pages.text: Day Five: THUNK! Blue box! Sapphire lens makes ocean-deep light swirl-whirl across the black sand. Day Six: CLUNK! Purple box tumbles down with an amethyst lens that creates royal-purple beams. Mango's tail coils and uncoils with growing excitement—his scales can't decide what color to be! Day Seven brings the biggest surprise: a clear crystal box containing... nothing? No, wait! Field Discovery: Inside sits a perfect triangular prism, just like the heart of his broken rainbow maker! The little chameleon's both eyes focus forward, examining this final gift. Could it be? Could all seven presents work together?

illustration.description: A chameleon with jewel-bright green-and-orange scales, independently swiveling eyes, a coiled tail, and sticky toes sits before seven opened boxes arranged in a rainbow pattern. The clear box in the center contains a triangular prism. All around him on the black volcanic sand are the seven colored lenses. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: All seven gift boxes arranged around Mango with the special prism in the center

pages.text: Final Experiment Log: Mango arranges all seven lenses in a careful circle around the crystal prism. His sticky toes grip the black sand as he positions each one just-right, just-so. The morning sun shines down, and... WHOOSH! Light enters the red lens, bounces to orange, leaps to yellow, dances to green, swooshes to blue, spirals to purple, and finally streams into the prism. And there—OH THERE!—a perfect rainbow arcs across the volcanic rim! Red-orange-yellow-green-blue-indigo-violet, shimmer-bright against the black sand and rising steam. The little chameleon's scales cycle through every color of his rainbow, faster and faster until he's practically glowing. 'I did it! I built it! I made it work!' Pop-pop-POP celebrate the steam vents. Research Conclusion: Sometimes when something beloved breaks, the pieces can become part of something even more wonderful. Seven gifts, seven days, one rainbow reborn—and one very happy chameleon scientist on the rim of a sleeping volcano.

illustration.description: A magnificent rainbow arcs across the volcanic landscape while a chameleon with jewel-bright green-and-orange scales, independently swiveling eyes, a coiled tail, and sticky toes sits in the center of his lens circle, scales rapidly cycling through all rainbow colors. The seven colored lenses and crystal prism work together on the black sand as steam vents puff happily. Golden age illustration, Arthur Rackham inspired, detailed pen work, watercolor washes, ornate fairy tale, timeless classic, decorative borders, enchanted scenes.
illustration.alt: Mango's rainbow creation success with the beautiful rainbow arcing overhead

Steam rises from volcanic vents in the background. coverPrompt.alt: Mango the chameleon surrounded by seven mysterious gifts on volcanic sand

questions.question: What broke at the beginning of the story?
questions.explanation: Mango's rainbow maker crystal broke into many pieces on the black sand.

questions.question: How many gifts arrived over the week?
questions.explanation: Seven gifts arrived, one each day for seven days, each containing a different colored lens.

questions.question: What was special about the last gift?
questions.explanation: The seventh gift contained a triangular prism, which was the key piece needed to make rainbows.

questions.question: Where does Mango live?
choices: On the rim of a sleeping volcano
questions.explanation: Mango lives on the black-sand rim of a sleeping volcano, with steam vents that go 'pop-pop-POP!'

questions.question: How did Mango make his new rainbow?
choices: He arranged seven lenses around a prism
questions.explanation: Mango arranged all seven colored lenses in a circle around the crystal prism to create his rainbow.
